message:
docs(db): correct datetime overflow comment range

## Minor Documentation Correction

**Change**: Updated OverflowError comment in datetime converter to reflect actual Python datetime range

### Before
```python
# - OverflowError: datetime value out of valid range (year 9999999+)
```

### After
```python
# - OverflowError: datetime value out of valid range (year outside 1-9999)
```

## Technical Accuracy

Python's `datetime.datetime` has a valid year range of **1 to 9999** (inclusive):
- Minimum: `datetime.datetime(1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0)`
- Maximum: `datetime.datetime(9999, 12, 31, 23, 59, 59, 999999)`

**OverflowError occurs when**:
- Year < 1 (e.g., year 0 or negative years)
- Year > 9999 (e.g., year 10000+)

The original comment only mentioned the high-end overflow (9999999+), but the error can occur on either end of the range.

## References

From Python documentation:
```python
>>> datetime.datetime.min
datetime.datetime(1, 1, 1, 0, 0)
>>> datetime.datetime.max
datetime.datetime(9999, 12, 31, 23, 59, 59, 999999)
```

## Impact

- **Functional**: None - this is a comment-only change
- **Documentation**: More accurate description of when OverflowError can occur
- **Code**: No changes to actual error handling logic

## Location

**File**: `src/mcp_agent_mail/db.py`
**Line**: 124 (in convert_datetime exception handling comment)
**Context**: SQLite datetime converter graceful degradation

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -121,7 +121,7 @@ def convert_datetime(val):
                 # - TypeError: unexpected type (shouldn't happen but defensive)
                 # - AttributeError: val has no expected attributes (defensive)
                 # - UnicodeDecodeError: corrupted bytes (extreme edge case)
-                # - OverflowError: datetime value out of valid range (year 9999999+)
+                # - OverflowError: datetime value out of valid range (year outside 1-9999)
                 return None
 
         # Register adapters globally (safe to call multiple times - last registration wins)
